articles.full_text:
Amravati Heat Wave: Temperature in Amravati district remains at 45.5 degrees for 10 consecutive days; Red alert for heat continues
The intensity of heat has increased across the state, with the heatwave intensifying day by day. The sun feels more intense during the afternoon hours. There is a possibility of further temperature increase in some areas. The heat has also impacted rural areas. Citizens are suffering due to the heat, making it difficult to go outside during the day. This extreme heat poses a risk to citizens' health. Many cities have reported temperatures exceeding 30 degrees. There is a forecast of further temperature rise, and some districts have been issued a red alert.
In this context, the temperature in Amravati district is increasing. Reports indicate that the temperature has remained at 45.5 degrees for 10 consecutive days, and the red alert for heat will remain in effect until tomorrow. Relief from the heat is expected only after May 26.
The red alert for heat will remain in Amravati district until tomorrow, with extreme heat being observed for the past 10 days. Citizens are urged to drink plenty of water and avoid going outside.
Short Summary
Temperature in Amravati remains at 45.5°C for 10 consecutive days
Red alert for heat continues until tomorrow
Some relief from the heat expected after May 26
Administration urges: Drink plenty of water, avoid going outside
Heat impact felt in towns and villages
